channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Bode George warns against using judiciary, INEC to destroy PDP
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Chief Olabode George warns against judicial manipulation and misuse of state institutions to destabilise the PDP ahead of the 2027 elections. Learn more ab
Read More: https://punchng.com/bode-george-warns-against-using-judiciary-inec-to-destroy-pdp/

item.title: Police arrest 10 suspected cultists over deadly Bayelsa attack
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Bayelsa Police have arrested 10 suspected cultists following a deadly attack in Yenagoa that killed one and injured three. Investigations into the Bayelsa
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-arrest-10-suspected-cultists-over-deadly-bayelsa-attack/

item.title: Man Utd sign Aston Villa midfielder Tielemans
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Manchester United announce the signing of Belgian midfielder Youri Tielemans from Aston Villa for £35 million. Read more about the Man Utd Tielemans deal.
Read More: https://punchng.com/man-utd-sign-aston-villa-midfielder-tielemans/

item.description:

Read More: https://punchng.com/nscdc-denies-shielding-officers-rift-with-security-agencies-in-akwa-ibom/

item.description:
The FCCPC has sealed three milk production factories in Anambra and arrested the owner over alleged non-compliance with acceptable production standards, po
Read More: https://punchng.com/fccpc-seals-three-milk-factories-arrests-owner-in-anambra/

item.title: Adeleke inaugurates roads, faculty buildings in UNILESA

item.description:
Governor Ademola Adeleke commissions new roads and faculty buildings at the University of Ilesa (UNILESA), fulfilling his pledge to develop the institution

item.title: Super Falcons to face Ghana in WAFCON friendly
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Super Falcons prepare for the 2026 WAFCON with a friendly against Ghana. Get ready for Nigeria’s quest for a record 11th title!

item.description:
Oladipupo Adebutu, PDP’s 2027 Ogun governorship candidate, welcomes defectors from APC, ADC, and other groups, rallying residents for PDP’s vision of prosp
Read More: https://punchng.com/2027-adebutu-receives-defectors-from-apc-adc-rallies-ogun-residents-for-pdp/

item.title: How Retired Worker’s Pension Rose from N18,000 to N206,000 After 21 Years – PenCom DG
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Director-General of the National Pension Commission (PenCom), Omolola Oloworaran, has highlighted the impact of recent pension reforms, revealing that a retired factory worker who had received a monthly pension of N18,000 for 21 years is now earning N206,000. Oloworaran shared the story while sp

item.title: Osun: Adeleke commissions major projects at UNILESA
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Osun State Governor, Ademola Adeleke, on Tuesday commissioned six major academic and infrastructural projects at the University of Ilesa (UNILESA), declaring that his administration had transformed the institution from a vision into a fast-growing centre of learning within three years. The governor,

item.title: Mexico files criminal complaints over deaths of citizens in US immigration custody
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Mexico has begun filing criminal complaints with state prosecutors in the United States over the deaths of its citizens in U.S. immigration... The post Mexico files criminal complaints over deaths of citizens in US immigration custody appeared first on Tribune Online.

This partnership signals a shift toward more accessi

item.title: Singer Boyposy Calls Out Carter Efe Over Alleged N3m Promotion Scam
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Berlin-based Nigerian singer, Omobolaji Ayanfeoluwa, popularly known as Boyposy, has publicly accused content creator and musician, Oderhohwo Joseph Efe, widely known as Carter Efe, of failing to deliver on a N3 million music promotion agreement.   Speaking in an interview with media outlet Onl

item.title: Fight misinformation, Customs CG urges 70 new NIPR inductees

item.description:
The Customs CG charges 70 new NIPR inductees to combat misinformation, strengthening strategic communication and public confidence in Customs operations.

item.title: Jackie Appiah’s Mum Just Gave Us a Gorgeous Kaba & Slit Look for Her 80th Birthday
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: Jackie Appiah's mother, Janet Owusu, marks her landmark 80th birthday in a stunning, custom-tailored Ghanaian Kaba and Slit featuring a vibrant, geometric Kente-inspired print. The post Jackie Appiah’s Mum Just Gave Us a Gorgeous Kaba & Slit Look for Her 80th Birthday appeared first on Be

item.title: Brace for $4 gas again: How U.S.-Iran tensions are threatening to end the price break at the pump
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Americans got some relief at the gas pump in recent weeks, enough to slow down inflation. That might not last long as the U.S. and Iran continue to vie for control of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Gov Fintiri Lauds Outgoing Council Chairmen, Urges Support For APC In 2027
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: In preparation to conclude two year tenure of the elected twenty one Local Government Areas Chairmen, Governor Ahmadu Umaru Fintiri, Tuesday hosted a valedictory session in their honour. The event which took place at the Government House, Yola is less than 24 hours before the expiration of the chair

item.title: Edo: Security operatives neutralise suspect, arrest another over 300-level AAU student’s abduction, killing
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The combined operatives of the Edo State Police Command, the Nigerian Army, the Edo State Security Corps, ESSC, and local hunters have neutralised one of the suspected kidnappers who killed a 24-year-old 300-level student of the Department of Anatomy, Ambrose Alli University, AAU, Ekpoma, identified

item.title: NURTW Lagos Organising Secretary, Ajiboye Dies Days After Alleged Gunmen Attack
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Organising Secretary of the National Union of Road Transport Workers (NURTW), Lagos State Council, Comrade Toba Ajiboye, has died, days after he was reportedly attacked by unidentified gunmen. Ajiboye’s death was confirmed by the Chairman of the NURTW Lagos State Council, Mustapha Adio Ade

item.title: CDS Reaffirms Commitment To Veterans Welfare
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Chief of Defence Staff (CDS), General Olufemi Oluyede, has reaffirmed commitment to veterans welfare in honour of their service to the nation. He made the commitment at a one – day strategic retreat with the leadership of various Military Veterans’ Associations at the Army Headquarte

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Minister of State for Petroleum Resources (Gas), Ekperikpe Ekpo, has described the Dangote Petroleum Refinery & Petrochemicals as a source of national pride and Nigeria’s most transformative industrial investments. He added the Federal Government will continue to provide the policy and

item.title: Turkish Club Gençlerbirliği Sack Super Eagles Winger
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Turkish Super Lig club Genclerbirligi have mutually agreed to terminate the contract of Super Eagles winger Henry Onyekuru, reports Completesports.com. The decision follows talks between the club and the 29-year-old Nigerian’s representatives, with both parties reaching an agreement to end his

item.title: Court grants former Taraba Governor Darius Ishaku permission to travel abroad on health grounds
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The judge adjourned hearing until 5 October, ordering Mr Ishaku to return to the country before then. The post Court grants former Taraba Governor Darius Ishaku permission to travel abroad on health grounds appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: PDP Reps candidate distributes free cooking gas to 600 Abeokuta South residents
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Amuzu, however, maintained that humanitarian support alone could not solve Nigeria's economic challenges, describing such interventions as temporary measures to provide relief while advocating long-term solutions through sound public policies.

item.title: Senate passes three House concurrence bills, advances FMC Arugungu bill
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The Senate considered and unanimously approved the three bills for concurrence at the Committee of the Whole on Tuesday. The post Senate passes three House concurrence bills, advances FMC Arugungu bill appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: AI-Generated Contents: Japan Approves New Laws to Tackle Election Misinformation on Social Media
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Japan’s National Diet has approved amendments to election and information laws aimed at curbing the spread of misinformation on social media platforms during elections. The revised Public Offices Election Law and the Information Distribution Platform Law were passed on Monday with backing from both

item.title: Highlights: India ease to victory over England in opening ODI
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Highlights of the first ODI at Edgbaston as India beat England by six wickets to take a 1-0 lead in the three-match series.

item.title: CSOs reel out 15-point roadmap to push for “Nigeria First”, others
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Civil society groups under CSCHEI unveil a 15-point roadmap focused on unity, security, food sufficiency, climate finance and governance. The post CSOs reel out 15-point roadmap to push for “Nigeria First”, others appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Lassa Fever: 4 States Record 31 New Cases As Death Toll Hits 221
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigeria Centre for Disease Control and Prevention (NCDC) has confirmed 31 new cases of Lassa fever in four states, pushing the country’s cumulative caseload to 922 and the death toll to 221. NCDC’s Lassa Fever Situation Report for Epidemiological Week 26 (June 22–28, 2026), revealed

item.title: What the Oyo situation teaches about Nigeria’s emerging security doctrine, By Crispin Oduobuk
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: In the years ahead, Nigeria’s greatest security victories may not be defined by the loudest battles. They will be defined by whether the institutions charged with protecting the nation continue learning to think, plan, and act as one… That is the enduring lesson from Oyo. And if sustained, it

item.title: PenCom: Police Exit From Contributory Pension Scheme Unnecessary
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The management of the National Pension Commission (PenCom) has said that the agitation by personnel of the Nigeria Police Force to exit the Contributory Pension Scheme (CPS) is unnecessary. The Director-General of PenCom, Omolola Oloworaran, disclosed this during an interaction with State House corr

item.title: N’East APC Youth Parliament Pledges Mobilisation For Tinubu/Shettima Ticket
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The APC Youth Parliament has pledged to mobilise support across the North-East for the re-election of President Bola Ahmed Tinubu and Vice President Kashim Shettima in the 2027 general elections. Speaking in Bauchi, the Chairman of the group, Kabiru Garba Kobi, said the retention of Shettima as the

item.title: Adebutu to make stable power top priority, says PDP Assembly candidate
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: PDP Assembly candidate Adeoye says an Adebutu administration will prioritise stable electricity to drive economic growth and industrial development in Ogun State.

item.description: INEC Chairman Joash Amupitan seeks improved funding and staff welfare support from RMAFC ahead of the 2027 general elections. The post INEC chairman decries poor funding, staff welfare appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: History Will Judge You By Your Courage, Not Your Laws– Shehu Sani Tells Lawmakers

item.description: Former Kaduna Central Senator, Shehu Sani, has urged members of the National Assembly to recognise that history will judge them not by the number of laws they pass or oversight functions they perform, but by the principled positions they take on issues of national importance. item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Ukraine’s Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko, has resigned from office as President Volodymyr Zelensky moves to restructure his government amid what he described as “new challenges and new tasks” facing the country. item.title: Lucid Motors denies report it’s considering bankruptcy
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The company said the "rumors are completely false" after its stock sank more than 50% on a report that it was weighing the option.

item.description: The Lagos State Police Command has uncovered a criminal syndicate accused of defrauding vehicle dealers of exotic vehicles worth more than N1.02bn, with detectives recovering several of the stolen vehicles and launching a manhunt for fleeing suspects. item.title: Iran Condemns UK Plan To Criminalise Support For IRGC
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Iran has condemned the United Kingdom’s plan to criminalise support for the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), describing the move as “unjustified, irresponsible and contrary to international law.” The Iranian Foreign Ministry, in a statement issued on Tuesday, criticised the proposed legisla

item.title: Poor funding may cripple NCDC’s mandate, Senate warns FG

item.description: Senate warns that inadequate funding could hinder NCDC operations, saying the commission’s N2.9bn monthly allocation is insufficient for its 2026 budget. The post Poor funding may cripple NCDC’s mandate, Senate warns FG appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: CBN repays N2.97 trillion OMO bills after N2.5 trillion auctions on Monday
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) repaid N2.97 trillion in Open Market Operations (OMO) bills maturities on Tuesday, a day after conducting N600 billion auctions where it allotted N2.54 trillion across the three tenors. item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Lagos State Government has ramped up its enforcement of environmental laws with the arrest of 56-year-old John Ehidreme for allegedly dumping refuse into a drainage channel in Ikorodu. item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: IBM’s stock plunges after a preliminary release of profit and revenue that were well below expectations.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) and UNICEF have raised fresh concerns over childhood immunisation in Nigeria and across Africa, warning that the region remains off track to recover from pandemic-era setbacks, leaving millions of children vulnerable to preventable diseases. item.description: Chelsea agree a deal worth about £850,000 to sign Manchester United striker Melvine Malard.

item.description: Home prices exploded over the past five years. They’re now going up more slowly, but incomes have still not kept up.

item.title: War, talks, repeat: How to break the cycle between US and Iran?
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Who needs nuclear weapons when you’ve got the Strait of Hormuz? Sure, ordinary Iranians are far worse off than on February 28th when the US and Israel launched a war that killed the Supreme Leader on Day One but the Islamic Republic's since discovered it’s got leverage beyond its wildest dream and b

item.title: Trump pivots from 20 percent Strait of Hormuz fee amid Iran war escalation
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Donald Trump backs away from fee, says regional countries will instead invest in US in return for transit protection.

item.title: Bundesliga vs Serie A: Ademola Lookman breaks down why one is tougher
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Super Eagles and Atletico Madrid forward Ademola Lookman has revealed the differences between the German Bundesliga and the Italian Serie A, Soccernet.ng reports. While the English Premier League is often regarded as the elite among Europe's top five, Lookman found it hard to break through in Englan

item.title: Derision at US chain Chipotle’s plan to sell its Mexican food in Mexico
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: One social media user described the opening of its first outlet in Monterrey as ‘like the dog teaching a duck to fly’A US chain’s plan to sell its version of Mexican food to Mexicans with a first branch south of the border has prompted bemusement, skepticism and anger among local people.Chipotle Mex

item.title: 2027: Don’t Help Tinubu Undermine Nigeria’s Democracy — ADC Tells Judiciary
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has accused the judiciary of aiding efforts to undermine Nigeria’s democracy, urging judges to remain independent amid ongoing legal disputes involving the opposition party. The party’s Deputy National Financial Secretary, Oladimeji Fabiyi, made the remarks on T

item.title: These are the obstacles still keeping women from retirement security
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Yes, women still need a “retirement security day.” Here’s why.

item.title: Biggest cover-up – Sowore speaks on arrest of fake PFIPC DG, Adeniyi Adeyemi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Human rights activist and former presidential candidate, Omoyele Sowore, has described the arrest of the self-acclaimed Director-General of the non-existent Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi, as “the biggest cover-up ever.” Sowore made th

item.title: FG Launches National Food Procurement Policy To Promote Healthier Diets
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Federal Government has launched new National Guidelines for the Public Procurement of Food and Related Services aimed at promoting healthier diets, improving nutrition and reducing the burden of diet-related diseases across Nigeria. The post FG Launches National Food Procurement Policy To Promot

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: After years of struggling to gain ground in Nigeria’s fiercely competitive telecommunications market, ntel is abandoning the traditional telecom operator read more ntel abandons telco playbook to chase AI, infrastructure growth

item.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr Leon Moosavi, Sociologist of race and religion, and Reader in the Department of Sociology, Social Policy and Criminology at the University of Liverpool. item.title: 'Back to the bar!' | Fan spills drink trying to catch Axar six!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Axar Patel's six for India makes a crowd member fall over and spill their drink trying to make the catch from the stands.

item.title: Natasha seeks mental support for rescued Oyo students, teachers
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Senator Natasha Akpoti-Uduaghan calls for trauma counselling and rehabilitation for rescued Oyo students, teachers after captivity ordeal. The post Natasha seeks mental support for rescued Oyo students, teachers appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: How Women Use Steroids To Fake Pregnancies, Deceive Husbands – Ex-NAPTIP DG
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Director-General of the National Agency for the Prohibition of Trafficking in Persons (NAPTIP), Julie Okah-Donli, has alleged that some women in Nigeria fake pregnancies by using steroids and later obtain babies to convince their husbands they had given birth. Okah-Donli made the claims in a

item.title: NBA election: Its time for members to freely choose their president – Boma Alabi

item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has rejected an application by Omoyele Sowore, Presidential candidate of the African Action Congress read more Court rejects Sowore’s adjournment request, fixes July 16 for hearing
